title: ai-harness-toolset reviewer config
description: Schema and key reference for config/reviewer.json. The reviewer config file itself must stay pure JSON with no comments; this adjacent schema carries the per-key documentation. Only currently implemented runtime keys are described. Per-key resolution: 'model' is REQUIRED and has NO built-in fallback — review-run.ps1 and review-safety-negtest.ps1 fail-fast if it is absent or empty (a concrete model version is tied to an external lifecycle and must come from this source-of-truth; precedence: explicit -Model CLI parameter > matched categoryPolicy entry 'model' > this scalar value > fail-fast). 'reasoningEffort' is optional and resolves explicit -Effort CLI parameter > matched categoryPolicy entry 'reasoningEffort' > this scalar value > policy safe-default 'xhigh'. 'categoryPolicy' is an optional config-backed category->{model,reasoningEffort} lookup selected per-invocation by review-run.ps1 -EffortCategory (U9). NOTE: review-run.ps1 reads this config as plain JSON and does NOT validate it against this schema at runtime — the schema documents intent; runtime enforcement is the precedence/validation logic in review-run.ps1 (an out-of-enum reasoningEffort, scalar or category, fails fast there).

model.description: Reviewer model. REQUIRED. Read by review-run.ps1 (and review-safety-negtest.ps1) and passed to the Codex CLI as --model. Precedence: explicit -Model CLI parameter > matched categoryPolicy entry 'model' > this scalar value > fail-fast. There is NO built-in model default/fallback: a concrete model version is tied to an external lifecycle, so an absent/empty model fails fast rather than being masked by a hardcoded version. fallbackModel is not auto-used.

reasoningEffort.description: Per-invocation reviewer reasoning effort (scalar default). Read by review-run.ps1 and passed to the Codex CLI as -c model_reasoning_effort=<value> when invoking the reviewer (enforced as of Batch B of the review-system polishing implementation plan; previously metadata-only). Precedence: explicit -Effort CLI parameter > matched categoryPolicy entry 'reasoningEffort' > this scalar value > built-in safe default 'xhigh'. The safe default is 'xhigh' per the adopted decision record (docs/systems/review/REVIEW_POLISHING_DECISION_RECORD.md); only clearly-simple local-correctness packets downgrade via an explicit -Effort or a tuned categoryPolicy entry. An out-of-enum value fails fast in review-run (no silent fallback). This config-driven enforcement does not by itself establish U9 operational, which also requires the Batch C reviewer-safety verification.

categoryPolicy.description: Optional config-backed review category policy: a map from a review category key to a {model, reasoningEffort} entry (U9 category->{model,effort} mapping lookup, decision record 'Script / config candidate decisions'). Selected per-invocation by review-run.ps1 -EffortCategory <key>; the operator chooses the category explicitly (judgment), the config supplies the mechanical {model,effort} for it (lookup), and review-run applies it. There is NO automatic category inference (not from changed files, not from -Stage, not LLM-based). A supplied category that is NOT present here is a soft miss: review-run falls back to the scalar 'model' / 'reasoningEffort' above (observable as effort-policy-match: missed), it does not fail hard. The schema portability contract: the category KEY set and the per-entry VALUES are per-project tunable (additionalProperties is a strict categoryEntry), while the canonical keys below are the documented starting set. The canonical set is intentionally GENERIC (universal software change classes), so the same global-install config is portable across projects; a project may add its own project-specific keys, but the shipped set carries none. SAFETY: this batch ships every canonical entry at the safe default (latest model + xhigh); per-category effort/model VALUE tuning below the safe default is deliberately deferred until first-cycle operational data (decision record U9: '세부 category 는 운용 데이터 후').

default.description: Ambiguous / context-important review, or an explicit request for the documented default tier. Safe default; ambiguity resolves here. NOTE: a category MISS does not fall back to this entry — it falls back to the scalar 'model' / 'reasoningEffort'. This entry is used only when -EffortCategory default is passed explicitly.

simple-local.description: Clearly-simple local-correctness packet (single file, one-line code/sentence change, docs typo). The only class the decision record allows to downgrade below the safe default — but this batch ships it at the safe default; downgrading is a deliberate future value-tuning step.

medium-scope.description: Mid-size, non-structural single-subsystem change (e.g. ~2 docs, single subsystem non-structural edit).

complex-broad.description: Broad/deep review: whole-turn review, delete/rename/restructure, skill/contract/script change, multi-subsystem. Downgrade-forbidden class.

system-coherence-heavy.description: system coherence review-heavy work (cross-doc/contract/skill/script/test coherence). Downgrade-forbidden class.

contract-sensitive.description: Contract-sensitive change: an API / interface / schema / machine-readable-output / parser / validator-gate contract that other code or consumers depend on. Downgrade-forbidden class (decision record §169 lists parser / machine-readable output / verdict contract under this class).

boundary-sensitive.description: Security / permission / sandbox / credentials / global·user config boundary-sensitive change. Downgrade-forbidden class.

script-runtime.description: Runtime / executable code behavior change (the program's own logic, not its tests or docs).

mechanical-audit.description: Mechanical / inspection-only audit closeout (no behavior change).

docs-wording.description: Docs-only wording / clarification change (no contract-meaning change).

categoryEntry.description: A single review category policy entry. 'reasoningEffort' is required; 'model' is optional (when omitted, review-run falls back to the scalar config 'model' for that category — a deliberate simplicity choice so a project can tune effort alone). Strict shape: no other keys.

model.description: Optional per-category reviewer model. When present and non-empty, review-run uses it (model-source: category) instead of the scalar 'model'. When absent, the scalar 'model' applies. A concrete model version is never hardcoded in a script — it lives here / in the scalar 'model'.

reasoningEffort.description: Per-category reviewer reasoning effort. Required. Passed to the Codex CLI as -c model_reasoning_effort=<value> when this category is selected and no explicit -Effort overrides it (effort-source: category). An out-of-enum value fails fast in review-run (source: category), same as the scalar path.
